title: Trump disapproval reaches new high, Post-ABC-Ipsos poll finds
description: Democrats now hold a five-point advantage in support for Congress, up from two points in February.

title: Drugmakers ask Supreme Court to restore abortion pill access by mail
description: The U.S. Court of Appeals for the 5th Circuit ruled that the commonly used abortion drug mifepristone can only be picked up in-person.

title: Germany says U.S. troop withdrawal 'anticipated', Spain and Italy could be next
description: Germany's defense minister is playing down the impact of the Pentagon's decision to pull 5,000 troops from the country, but the move has rattled NATO allies and added to growing fears that Europe can no longer rely on Washington.

title: Iran submits 14-point response to U.S. proposal to end war
description: Iran has presented a 14-point response to the U.S. proposal to end the war, according to Iranian state media.

title: Spirit Airlines, low-cost innovator, shuts down after Trump bailout plan fails
description: The airline said early Saturday it had “no choice” but to wind down operations immediately, dashing hopes of a last-minute financial lifeline.

title: Hegseth tightens control at Pentagon, defiant and more confident than ever
description: Trump’s combative defense secretary has grown more self-assured of his job security despite his early missteps in the role, people familiar with the matter said.

title: Pentagon to pull 5,000 troops from Germany, alarming Republican lawmakers
description: NATO said it was working to understand details of the plan to draw down about 5,000 troops, which coincides with a feud between the president and the German chancellor.

title: Diagnosed with colon cancer at 36, she spent her last years helping others with the disease
description: The daughter of Iranian immigrants, Asal Sayas worked in the White House and Senate, lobbied for AIDS research and became a tireless champion for people with cancer.

title: Trump is running out of options to contain gas price backlash
description: The rising cost of oil is becoming a major political concern for the White House, as the stalemate between President Donald Trump and Iran drags on.

title: The surprising history of Black representation in Congress
description: It took the Voting Rights Act in 1965, and its revisions decades later, to restore Black congressional representation in the South after Reconstruction.

title: Week in Politics Hegseth's Congressional testimony; SCOTUS and Voting Rights
description: We look at Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth's two-day Congressional testimony, as well as what the Supreme Court's changes to the Voting Rights Act will mean for future elections.

title: How are Trump's efforts to reshape Indiana's congressional districts going?
description: President Trump is using his influence to run Republican challengers against some of the Indiana state senators who refused to redistrict to keep GOP control of the U.S. House.

title: Can Trump's latest pick for surgeon general make it through confirmation?
description: Nicole Saphier, a breast cancer radiologist, is the president's third nominee for surgeon general. Will she get confirmed?

title: 2026 Senate races to watch: From most likely to flip to Democratic long shots
description: Democrats could have a narrow path to winning the Senate, with these specific states on the table.

title: Trump endorses Andy Barr in race to succeed Mitch McConnell
description: Trump also asked one of Barr’s rivals to drop out of the Republican primary in Kentucky, where the winner of that race is likely to succeed McConnell.

title: Two more Southern states move toward adding Republican House seats
description: GOP-led states are rushing to take advantage of the Supreme Court’s decision to curtail the Voting Rights Act with new maps that could end the careers of several Black Democratic House members.

title: Trump returns to public events, delivering profane speech
description: In his first public speech since the White House correspondents’ dinner, the president mocked transgender weightlifters and the “medical crap” an adviser was telling him.

title: Pirro says video shows moment gunman fired outside correspondents’ dinner
description: Defense attorneys for the alleged gunman have questioned the evidence in the case, and the U.S. attorney’s X posts go beyond what prosecutors have said in court filings.

title: Trump says Iran conflict is ‘terminated’ as he hits congressional deadline
description: Trump’s statement came as the United States continues to enforce a naval blockade of Iran and as he declined to rule out additional strikes on the country.

title: Ex-Fla. congressman and Rubio ally convicted of secretly lobbying for Venezuela
description: Prosecutors had alleged that David Rivera had received a secret $50 million contract to influence the first Trump administration.

title: After a Supreme Court ruling, expect even more gerrymandering
description: It's felt like a head-spinning week on the topic of redistricting. We take stock after a major Supreme Court ruling and yet another state passing a new congressional map.

title: Tracking who Trump is appointing to fill key administration roles
description: Follow President Trump’s progress filling over 800 positions, among about 1,300 that require Senate confirmation, in this tracker from The Washington Post and the Partnership for Public Service.

title: Medicare portal database exposed health providers’ Social Security numbers
description: It is the latest technical problem related to the Trump administration’s push to create a national directory of health providers.

title: A deadline for the Iran war is here. What does the War Powers Act say?
description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth asserted an ongoing ceasefire “pauses” the clock on the 60-day deadline to end hostilities in Iran or seek congressional approval.

title: Poll: Trump’s Iran war reaches Iraq- and Vietnam-era disapproval levels
description: A Post-ABC-Ipsos poll shows most Americans call the military action a mistake, even as Republicans remain strongly supportive. Many fear the conflict will lead to a recession.

title: Trump said he would end her political career, but Democrats did her in
description: Liberals say they want a fighter. But Janet Mills, a popular governor who clashed with the president, failed to catch fire in Maine’s Senate race.

title: A long-standing Senate tradition is being quietly undermined
description: Senate Majority Leader John Thune has resisted President Donald Trump’s pleas to kill the filibuster, but Republicans circumvented it to try to fund immigration enforcement agencies.

title: Stalemate with Iran puts Trump's second term to the test
description: President Trump's stalemate in Iran spells trouble for the rest of his second term.

title: Republican strategist on how the Iran war is affecting Trump politically
description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Republican strategist Rina Shah about the political ramifications of the war in Iran for President Trump.

title: It's Day 1 of Medicaid work requirements in Nebraska. People are worried
description: Starting May 1, many people covered by Medicaid in Nebraska have to prove they are working. It's a requirement most states will have to implement under President Trump's budget law, beginning in January.
